data.body:
Here, a million is 10⁶, but a billion is a thousand million (10⁹), and a trillion is a thousand billion (10¹²). Each new named unit represents a factor of a thousand more than the previous one.

The most commonly encountered difference is with the word "billion.
data.author: did:plc:s6rybulfgm6ctnf23x6whepu

The ambiguity of "billion" is, however, a frequent source of misunderstanding in common discourse.
